docs.title: Haxe Addons
docs.text: A curated collection of useful addons, utilities, and experimental modules for the Haxe programming language.

docs.title: Installation
docs.text:
haxelib git haxe-addons https://github.com/Paopun20/haxe-addons

Then include it in your project.

Base HaxeLime | OpenFL 
-lib haxe-addons

<haxelib name="haxe-addons" />

docs.title: Deque
docs.text:
A double-ended queue (deque) implementation with optional max-length bound.

import haxe.addons.collections.Deque;

docs.title: Constructor
docs.text:
new(?maxlen:Int = 0)

Creates an empty deque. If maxlen > 0, the deque is bounded — pushing beyond the limit evicts elements from the opposite end.

docs.title: Properties
docs.text: Property Type Description length Int Number of elements

docs.title: append(value:T):Void
docs.text: Push to the right end. Evicts leftmost element if bounded and full.

docs.title: appendLeft(value:T):Void
docs.text: Push to the left end. Evicts rightmost element if bounded and full.

docs.title: pop():Null<T>
docs.text: Remove and return the rightmost element. Returns null if empty.

docs.title: popLeft():Null<T>
docs.text: Remove and return the leftmost element. Returns null if empty.

docs.title: iterator():Iterator<T>
docs.text: Iterate from left to right.

docs.title: fromArray<T>(array:Array<T>):Deque<T>
docs.text: Create a deque from an existing array.

docs.title: Operators
docs.text: Operator Description deque[index] Array-access read deque[index] = value Array-access write for (item in deque) Iterator String(deque) String representation

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
var d = new Deque<Int>(3);
d.append(1);
d.append(2);
d.append(3);
d.append(4); // evicts 1
trace(d.popLeft()); // 2

docs.title: Interpolation
docs.text:
Static utility class providing common interpolation and easing functions.

import haxe.addons.math.Interpolation;

docs.title: lerp(a:Float, b:Float, t:Float):Float
docs.text: Linear interpolation between a and b by t.

docs.title: clamp(t:Float, min:Float = 0, max:Float = 1):Float
docs.text: Clamp t to the range [min, max].

docs.title: smoothStep(edge0:Float, edge1:Float, v:Float):Float
docs.text: 3rd-order Hermite interpolation (smoothstep).

docs.title: smootherStep(edge0:Float, edge1:Float, v:Float):Float
docs.text: 5th-order smoother interpolation.

docs.title: easeInQuad(t:Float):Float
docs.text: Quadratic ease-in.

docs.title: easeOutQuad(t:Float):Float
docs.text: Quadratic ease-out.

docs.title: easeInOutQuad(t:Float):Float
docs.text: Quadratic ease-in-out.

docs.title: easeInCubic(t:Float):Float
docs.text: Cubic ease-in.

docs.title: easeOutCubic(t:Float):Float
docs.text: Cubic ease-out.

docs.title: easeInOutCubic(t:Float):Float
docs.text: Cubic ease-in-out.

docs.title: exponential(t:Float, power:Float = 2):Float
docs.text: Exponential tween.

docs.title: bounce(t:Float):Float
docs.text: Bounce easing — simulates a bouncing ball.

docs.title: pingPong(t:Float):Float
docs.text: Oscillates t between 0 and 1 in a sawtooth pattern (0 → 1 → 0 → 1 → ...).

docs.title: MathTypes
docs.text:
Common math-related type aliases.

import haxe.addons.math.MathTypes;

docs.title: Vector2<T = Float>
docs.text:
typedef Vector2<T = Float> = { x:T; y:T; }

A 2D vector structure.

docs.title: Vector3<T = Float>
docs.text:
typedef Vector3<T = Float> = { x:T; y:T; z:T; }

A 3D vector structure.

Both default to Float components but can be used with any type:

var v2:Vector2<Int> = { x: 1, y: 2 };
var v3:Vector3 = { x: 1.0, y: 2.0, z: 3.0 };

docs.title: Noise API
docs.text:
Facade class that provides static shortcuts to all noise generation algorithms.

import haxe.addons.math.Noise;

docs.title: Cellular / Worley Noise
docs.text: Method Return cellularSample2D(x, y, seed=0, jitter=1.0) Float cellularEdge2D(x, y, seed=0, jitter=1.0) Float cellularSample3D(x, y, z, seed=0, jitter=1.0) Float cellularEdge3D(x, y, z, seed=0, jitter=1.0) Float

docs.title: Curl Noise
docs.text: Method Return curlSample2D(noise, x, y, strength=1.0, seed=0) Vector2 curlSimplex(x, y, strength=1.0, seed=0) Vector2 curlFbm(x, y, strength=1.0, octaves=5, seed=0) Vector2

docs.text: Method Return fractalFbm(noise, x, y, octaves=5, persistence=0.5, lacunarity=2.0, seed=0) Float fractalBillow(noise, x, y, octaves=5, persistence=0.5, lacunarity=2.0, seed=0) Float fractalRidged(noise, x, y, octaves=5, persistence=0.5, lacunarity=2.0, seed=0) Float

docs.title: CellularNoise
docs.text:
Cellular (Voronoi) noise — nearest-distance-to-cell-center sampling.

import haxe.addons.math.noises.CellularNoise;

docs.title: Methods
docs.text: Method Description sample2D(x:Float, y:Float, seed:Int=0, jitter:Float=1.0):Float Distance to nearest cell center edge2D(x:Float, y:Float, seed:Int=0, jitter:Float=1.0):Float F2 - F1 edge detection sample3D(x:Float, y:Float, z:Float, seed:Int=0, jitter:Float=1.0):Float 3D distance to nearest cell center edge3D(x:Float, y:Float, z:Float, seed:Int=0, jitter:Float=1.0):Float 3D edge detection

docs.title: CurlNoise
docs.text:
Curl noise — generates divergence-free vector fields from scalar noise inputs. Useful for fluid-like motion and particle systems.

import haxe.addons.math.noises.CurlNoise;

docs.title: Methods
docs.text:
static function sample2D(noise:NoiseFunction, x:Float, y:Float, strength:Float = 1.0, seed:Int = 0):Vector2

Compute 2D curl from an arbitrary scalar noise function.

static function simplex(x:Float, y:Float, strength:Float = 1.0, seed:Int = 0):Vector2

Curl using simplex noise as the scalar field.

static function fbm(x:Float, y:Float, strength:Float = 1.0, octaves:Int = 5, seed:Int = 0):Vector2

Curl from an FBM-combined simplex noise field.

docs.title: DomainWarp
docs.text:
Domain warping — distorts the input coordinates by a noise field before sampling, creating organic, swirling patterns.

import haxe.addons.math.noises.DomainWarp;

docs.title: Methods
docs.text:
static function warp2D(noise:NoiseFunction, warpNoise:NoiseFunction, x:Float, y:Float, strength:Float = 1.0, seed:Int = 0):Float

Generic domain warping using two noise functions.

static function simplexWarp(x:Float, y:Float, strength:Float = 1.0, seed:Int = 0):Float

Domain warp using simplex noise.

static function fbmWarp(x:Float, y:Float, strength:Float = 1.0, octaves:Int = 5, seed:Int = 0):Float

Domain warp using FBM simplex noise.

docs.title: Fractal
docs.text:
Fractal noise combiners — layer multiple octaves of a base noise function.

import haxe.addons.math.noises.Fractal;

docs.title: Methods
docs.text:
static function fbm(noise:NoiseFunction, x:Float, y:Float, octaves:Int = 5, persistence:Float = 0.5, lacunarity:Float = 2.0, seed:Int = 0):Float

Standard Fractional Brownian Motion.

static function billow(noise:NoiseFunction, x:Float, y:Float, octaves:Int = 5, persistence:Float = 0.5, lacunarity:Float = 2.0, seed:Int = 0):Float

Billow fractal (abs value of noise before weighting).

static function ridged(noise:NoiseFunction, x:Float, y:Float, octaves:Int = 5, persistence:Float = 0.5, lacunarity:Float = 2.0, seed:Int = 0):Float

Ridged fractal (1 - abs(noise)) for sharp ridge-like features.

docs.title: NoiseMap
docs.text:
Generate and manipulate noise maps — 2D/3D grids sampled from a noise function.

import haxe.addons.math.noises.NoiseMap;

docs.title: Methods
docs.text:
static function build2D(width:Int, height:Int, noise:Noise2D, scale:Float = 1.0, xOff:Float = 0.0, yOff:Float = 0.0, seed:Int = 0):Array<Array<Float>>

Generate a 2D noise map of size width × height.

static function build3D(width:Int, height:Int, depth:Int, noise:Noise3D, scale:Float = 1.0, xOff:Float = 0.0, yOff:Float = 0.0, zOff:Float = 0.0, seed:Int = 0):Array<Array<Array<Float>>>

Generate a 3D noise map of size width × height × depth.

docs.title: NoiseTypes
docs.text:
Common type aliases for noise function signatures.

import haxe.addons.math.noises.NoiseTypes;

docs.title: Type Aliases
docs.text:
typedef NoiseFunction = Float -> Float -> Int -> Float;
typedef Noise2D = Float -> Float -> Int -> Float;
typedef Noise3D = Float -> Float -> Float -> Int -> Float;

All take (x, y [, z], seed) and return a Float in [-1, 1].

  • NoiseFunction / Noise2D — 2D noise: (x, y, seed) → value
  • Noise3D — 3D noise: (x, y, z, seed) → value

docs.title: OpenSimplex2
docs.text:
OpenSimplex2 noise implementation — improved simplex noise with fewer directional artifacts.

import haxe.addons.math.noises.OpenSimplex2;

docs.title: Methods
docs.text: Method Description sample2D(x:Float, y:Float, seed:Int=0):Float 2D noise sample sample3D(x:Float, y:Float, z:Float, seed:Int=0):Float 3D noise sample fbm2D(x, y, octaves=5, persistence=0.5, lacunarity=2.0, seed=0):Float 2D FBM fbm3D(x, y, z, octaves=5, persistence=0.5, lacunarity=2.0, seed=0):Float 3D FBM

docs.title: PerlinNoise
docs.text:
Classic Perlin noise implementation.

import haxe.addons.math.noises.PerlinNoise;

docs.title: Methods
docs.text: Method Description sample2D(x:Float, y:Float, seed:Int=0):Float 2D Perlin noise sample fbm2D(x, y, octaves=5, persistence=0.5, lacunarity=2.0, seed=0):Float 2D FBM

docs.title: SimplexNoise
docs.text:
Simplex noise implementation.

import haxe.addons.math.noises.SimplexNoise;

docs.title: Methods
docs.text: Method Description sample2D(x:Float, y:Float, seed:Int=0):Float 2D simplex noise sample fbm2D(x, y, octaves=5, persistence=0.5, lacunarity=2.0, seed=0):Float 2D FBM

docs.title: ValueNoise
docs.text:
Value noise interpolates random lattice values for a smooth, grid-based noise.

import haxe.addons.math.noises.ValueNoise;

docs.title: Methods
docs.text: Method Description sample2D(x:Float, y:Float, seed:Int=0):Float 2D value noise sample fbm2D(x, y, octaves=5, persistence=0.5, lacunarity=2.0, seed=0):Float 2D FBM

docs.title: WorleyNoise
docs.text:
Worley (cellular) noise - distance to nearest random cell point.

import haxe.addons.math.noises.WorleyNoise;

docs.title: Methods
docs.text:
static function sample2D(x:Float, y:Float, seed:Int = 0):Float

Distance to nearest cell center point.

static function edge2D(x:Float, y:Float, seed:Int = 0):Float

F1 - F2 edge pattern (difference between nearest and second-nearest distance).

docs.title: Path
docs.text:
A pathlib-style abstraction for filesystem path manipulation.

import haxe.addons.system.Path;

docs.title: Constructor
docs.text:
new(path:String)

Creates a normalized Path from a string.

docs.title: Static Methods
docs.text: Method Description cwd():Path Current working directory home():Path User home directory

docs.title: Properties
docs.text: Property Type Description name String Final path component stem String Name without extension parent Path Logical parent directory parents Array<Path> All ancestor directories suffix String File extension (with dot) suffixes Array<String> All extensions (e.g. [".tar", ".gz"])

docs.title: Methods
docs.text: Method Description join(other:String):Path Join paths (via / operator) parts():Array<String> Split path into components isAbsolute():Bool Absolute path check withName(newName:String):Path Replace final component withSuffix(newSuffix:String):Path Replace extension resolve():Path Resolve to absolute exists():Bool Filesystem existence check isFile():Bool Regular file check isDir():Bool Directory check stat():sys.FileStat Filesystem metadata mkdir(parents=false, existOk=false):Void Create directory iterdir():Array<Path> List directory entries readText():String Read file as text writeText(content:String):Void Write text readBytes():haxe.io.Bytes Read file as bytes writeBytes(bytes:haxe.io.Bytes):Void Write bytes touch():Void Create empty file if absent unlink(missingOk=false):Void Delete file rmdir():Void Delete empty directory rename(target:String):Path Rename / move

docs.title: Operators
docs.text: Operator Description path / "subdir" Path joining path == other Equality comparison String(path) String representation

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
var p = new Path("/home/user/docs") / "file.txt";
trace(p.stem);     // "file"
trace(p.suffix);   // ".txt"
trace(p.parent);   // /home/user/docs

docs.title: Thread
docs.text:
Simple thread abstraction built on top of Haxe's sys.thread API.

import haxe.addons.thread.SimpleThreadPool;
import haxe.addons.thread.SimpleThread;

Only available on threaded targets (cpp, cs, hl, eval, python, lua, java, jvm).

docs.title: SimpleThreadPool
docs.text: A managed pool of worker threads backed by sys.thread.ElasticThreadPool.

docs.title: Constructor
docs.text:
new(maxThreadsCount:Int, threadTimeout:Float = 60)

Creates a pool with up to maxThreadsCount concurrent threads. Idle threads are terminated after threadTimeout seconds.

docs.title: Properties
docs.text: Property Type Description threadsCount Int Number of active threads (read-only) isShutdown Bool Whether the pool has been shut down

docs.title: Methods
docs.text: Method Description run(task:SimpleThread->Void):Void Enqueue a task, receives a SimpleThread ref shutdown():Void Shut down the pool and release all threads

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
final pool = new SimpleThreadPool(4);
pool.run(st -> {
    st.editGlobalVar(() -> {
        total += compute(id);
    });
});
pool.shutdown();

docs.title: SimpleThread
docs.text:
Handle passed to each task, providing safe access to shared state.

Instances are created internally by SimpleThreadPool — do not construct directly.

docs.title: Methods
docs.text: Method Description editGlobalVar(func:() -> Void):Void Execute func while holding the pool-wide mutex isGlobalLock():Bool Check if the pool-wide mutex is currently held by any thread

docs.title: editGlobalVar
docs.text: Acquires the pool's global mutex, runs func, then releases it. Any exception thrown by func is rethrown in the calling thread after the mutex is released.

docs.title: isGlobalLock
docs.text: Non-blocking check — returns true if another thread currently holds the global mutex, false otherwise.

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
pool.run(st -> {
    if (!st.isGlobalLock()) {
        st.editGlobalVar(() -> {
            sharedCounter++;
        });
    }
});

docs.title: ArrayTools
docs.text:
Static helper methods for arrays.

import haxe.addons.tools.ArrayTools;

docs.title: Methods
docs.text: Method Description shuffle<T>(arr:Array<T>):Array<T> Fisher-Yates shuffle (returns new array) forEach<T>(arr:Array<T>, f:T->Void):Void Inline iteration randomItem<T>(arr:Array<T>):Null<T> Random element first<T>(arr:Array<T>):Null<T> First element last<T>(arr:Array<T>):Null<T> Last element flatten<T>(arr:Array<Array<T>>):Array<T> Flatten nested arrays removeDuplicates<T>(arr:Array<T>):Array<T> Remove duplicate values isEmpty<T>(arr:Array<T>):Bool Check if empty clear<T>(arr:Array<T>):Array<T> Remove all elements sum(arr:Array<Float>):Float Arithmetic sum average(arr:Array<Float>):Float Arithmetic mean minOf(arr:Array<Float>):Float Minimum value maxOf(arr:Array<Float>):Float Maximum value chunk<T>(arr:Array<T>, size:Int):Array<Array<T>> Split into fixed-size chunks rotate<T>(arr:Array<T>, n:Int):Array<T> Left rotate by n positions count<T>(arr:Array<T>, pred:T->Bool):Int Count elements matching predicate any<T>(arr:Array<T>, pred:T->Bool):Bool Any element matches all<T>(arr:Array<T>, pred:T->Bool):Bool All elements match zip<A,B>(a:Array<A>, b:Array<B>):Array<{a:A,b:B}> Zip two arrays into pairs

docs.title: FloatTools
docs.text:
Static utility class for float operations.

import haxe.addons.tools.FloatTools;

docs.title: Methods
docs.text: Method Description clamp(t:Float, min:Float, max:Float):Float Clamp to range lerp(a:Float, b:Float, t:Float):Float Linear interpolation remap(v, inMin, inMax, outMin, outMax):Float Remap between ranges snap(v:Float, step:Float):Float Snap to nearest step sign(v:Float):Int Sign (-1, 0, 1) between(v, min, max):Bool Inclusive range check abs(v:Float):Float Absolute value toInt(v:Float):Int Truncate to integer roundTo(v:Float, decimals:Int):Float Round to N decimal places pingPong(v:Float, length:Float):Float Oscillate 0 → length → 0 → ... randomRange(min:Float, max:Float):Float Random float in range chance(percent:Float):Bool Percentage probability roll wrap(v:Int, min:Int, max:Int):Int Integer wrapping randomInt(min:Int, max:Int):Int Random integer in range smoothStep(edge0, edge1, v):Float 3rd-order smooth interpolation smootherStep(edge0, edge1, v):Float 5th-order smooth interpolation inverseLerp(a, b, v):Float Inverse linear interpolation deadzone(v, threshold):Float Zero within threshold approach(current, target, step):Float Step toward target without overshoot oscillate(time, freq, amplitude, offset=0):Float Sinusoidal oscillation toRad(deg:Float):Float Degrees to radians (deg * PI / 180) toDeg(rad:Float):Float Radians to degrees (rad * 180 / PI)

docs.title: IntTools
docs.text:
Static utility class for integer operations.

import haxe.addons.tools.IntTools;

docs.title: Methods
docs.text: Method Description inRange(v:Int, min:Int, max:Int):Bool Inclusive range check isEven(v:Int):Bool Check if even isOdd(v:Int):Bool Check if odd toFloat(v:Int):Float Convert to float digitCount(v:Int):Int Number of decimal digits gcd(a:Int, b:Int):Int Greatest common divisor lcm(a:Int, b:Int):Int Least common multiple

docs.title: StringTools
docs.text:
Static utility class for string operations.

import haxe.addons.tools.StringTools;

docs.title: Methods
docs.text: Method Description isNullOrEmpty(s:String):Bool Null or empty check capitalize(s:String):String Capitalize first letter trimTo(s:String, max:Int, ellipsis:String="..."):String Truncate with ellipsis padLeft(s:String, len:Int, char:String="0"):String Left-pad string padRight(s:String, len:Int, char:String=" "):String Right-pad string contains(s:String, sub:String):Bool Substring check repeat(s:String, times:Int):String Repeat string N times toIntSafe(s:String, fallback:Int=0):Int Safe integer parse toFloatSafe(s:String, fallback:Float=0):Float Safe float parse reverse(s:String):String Reverse string countOccurrences(s:String, sub:String):Int Count substring occurrences wordWrap(s:String, maxLen:Int):Array<String> Word-wrap text to lines

docs.title: BitField
docs.text:
An 8-bit bitfield abstraction backed by Int.

import haxe.addons.types.BitField;

docs.title: Constructor
docs.text:
new(value:Int = 0)

Creates a bitfield, masked to 8 bits (0xFF).

docs.title: Properties
docs.text: Property Type Description length Int Number of bits (8) this[index] Bool Get/set bit by index

docs.title: Methods
docs.text: Method Description set(bit:Int):Void Set bit to 1 clear(bit:Int):Void Clear bit to 0 toggle(bit:Int):Void Toggle bit has(bit:Int):Bool Check if bit is set setValue(bit:Int, value:Bool):Void Set bit to specific value reset():Void Clear all bits fill(value:Bool):Void Fill all bits toString():String Binary string (MSB first, e.g. 00101101)

docs.title: Operators
docs.text: Operator Description a \| b Bitwise OR a & b Bitwise AND a ^ b Bitwise XOR ~a Bitwise NOT Bool(value) True if any bit is set

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
var bf = new BitField();
bf.set(0);
bf.set(3);
trace(bf.has(0)); // true
trace(bf);        // "00001001"

docs.title: Int8
docs.text:
A signed 8-bit integer type with wrapping arithmetic.

import haxe.addons.types.Int8;

On supported targets (cpp, cs, java), this maps to the native Int8 type with bounds checking. On other targets it uses a wrapped Int under the hood.

docs.title: Constructor
docs.text:
new(value:Int = 0)

Creates an Int8 with the value normalized to [-128, 127].

docs.title: Operators
docs.text: Operator Description a + b Addition (wrapping) a - b Subtraction (wrapping) a * b Multiplication (wrapping) a / b Integer division a % b Modulo -a Negation a & b Bitwise AND a \| b Bitwise OR a ^ b Bitwise XOR a << b Left shift a >> b Right shift (arithmetic) Int(value) Implicit conversion to Int

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
var a:Int8 = 100;
var b:Int8 = 50;
trace(a + b); // -106 (wraps past 127)

docs.title: UInt8
docs.text:
An unsigned 8-bit integer type with wrapping arithmetic.

import haxe.addons.types.UInt8;

On supported targets (cpp, cs) this maps to the native UInt8 type. On other targets it uses a wrapped Int.

docs.title: Constructor
docs.text:
new(v:Int)

Creates a UInt8 with the value wrapped to [0, 255].

docs.title: Operators
docs.text: Operator Description a + b Addition (wrapping) a - b Subtraction (wrapping) a * b Multiplication (wrapping) a / b Integer division a % b Modulo -a Negation a & b Bitwise AND a \| b Bitwise OR a ^ b Bitwise XOR ~a Bitwise NOT a << b Left shift a >> b Right shift a == b / a != b Equality a < b / a <= b Less than a > b / a >= b Greater than

docs.title: Methods
docs.text: Method Description toUInt8():Int Unsigned integer view isNegative():Bool Sign check (always false for UInt8 in range) abs():UInt8 Absolute value clamp(lo:UInt8, hi:UInt8):UInt8 Clamp to range toString():String String representation Int(value) Implicit conversion to Int

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
var u:UInt8 = 200;
var v:UInt8 = 100;
trace(u + v); // 44 (wraps at 256)

docs.title: ArrayOfBabel
docs.text:
A self-referential infinite array — every element is itself.

import haxe.addons.useless.ArrayOfBabel;

new()

Creates an array where arr[0] == arr, arr[0][0] == arr, and so on infinitely.

static function fromArray(arr:Array<ArrayOfBabel>):ArrayOfBabel

docs.title: Operator
docs.text: Operator Description arr[index] Returns arr itself String(arr) Infinite recursion (use with caution)

docs.title: BlackHole
docs.text:
An array that consumes everything and returns nothing.

import haxe.addons.useless.BlackHole;

new()

docs.title: Methods
docs.text: Method Description push(value:T):Int Devours value, always returns 0 this[index] Always returns null this[index] = value Consumes value silently

docs.title: Properties
docs.text: Property Value length 0 (always)

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
var bh = new BlackHole<Int>();
bh.push(42);
bh.push(999);
trace(bh.length); // 0
trace(bh[0]);     // null

docs.title: QuantumBoolean
docs.text:
Schrödinger's boolean — simultaneously true and false until observed.

import haxe.addons.useless.QuantumBoolean;

new()

Initializes to true (the unobserved state).

docs.title: Methods
docs.text: Method Description observe():Bool "Measure" — collapses to a random boolean collapse():Bool Direct value read without random collapse entangle(other:QuantumBoolean):QuantumBoolean Entangle two quantum booleans

docs.title: Operators
docs.text: Operator Description Bool(value) Collapses to random boolean (observer effect)

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
var q = new QuantumBoolean();
trace(q.observe()); // true or false (random)
trace(q.observe()); // different each time
